title: Extend AgentResult with token fields
analysis: AgentResult is a dataclass in shared/src/shared/cli_invoke.py with fields: success, output, cost_usd, duration_ms, turns, session_id, structured_output, subtype, stderr. No token tracking exists. _parse_claude_output in the same file parses Claude CLI JSON but ignores the usage block. _parse_codex_output and _parse_gemini_output live in orchestrator/src/orchestrator/agents/invoke.py — they already compute total_input_tokens/total_output_tokens internally but discard them after cost estimation. The task adds 4 Optional[int] fields to AgentResult and wires all three parsers to populate them. Claude gets all 4 fields from usage.input_tokens, usage.output_tokens, usage.cache_read_input_tokens, usage.cache_creation_input_tokens. Codex/Gemini get input_tokens and output_tokens from existing internal accumulators; cache fields stay None. Tests for Claude parsing go in shared/tests/test_cli_invoke.py; tests for codex/gemini go in orchestrator/tests/test_invoke.py since shared cannot import orchestrator (would create circular dependency).

steps.description: Write test in shared/tests/test_cli_invoke.py: TestAgentResultTokenFields — verify AgentResult accepts input_tokens, output_tokens, cache_read_tokens, cache_create_tokens kwargs and that all four default to None when omitted. Also verify existing _make_result helper still works (backward compat).

Add four Optional[int] fields to the AgentResult dataclass in shared/src/shared/cli_invoke.py: input_tokens: int | None = None, output_tokens: int | None = None, cache_read_tokens: int | None = None, cache_create_tokens: int | None = None. Place them after the existing numeric fields (cost_usd, duration_ms, turns) for logical grouping.

Write test in shared/tests/test_cli_invoke.py: TestParseClaudeTokens — create a _SubprocessResult with stdout containing a Claude CLI JSON blob that includes a usage block {input_tokens: 1500, output_tokens: 800, cache_read_input_tokens: 300, cache_creation_input_tokens: 50}. Call _parse_claude_output and assert result.input_tokens == 1500, result.output_tokens == 800, result.cache_read_tokens == 300, result.cache_create_tokens == 50.

Update _parse_claude_output in shared/src/shared/cli_invoke.py: after parsing the JSON data dict, extract usage = data.get('usage', {}). Map usage.get('input_tokens') to input_tokens, usage.get('output_tokens') to output_tokens, usage.get('cache_read_input_tokens') to cache_read_tokens, usage.get('cache_creation_input_tokens') to cache_create_tokens. Pass these to the AgentResult constructor. Use None (not 0) as the default when a key is absent — this preserves the distinction between 'zero tokens' and 'not reported'.

Write test in shared/tests/test_cli_invoke.py: test_parse_claude_output_no_usage_block — create a _SubprocessResult with valid Claude JSON but no usage key. Call _parse_claude_output and assert all four token fields are None. This validates graceful degradation for older CLI versions.

Write test in orchestrator/tests/test_invoke.py: TestParseCodexTokens — create a _SubprocessResult with Codex JSONL output including turn.completed events with usage {input_tokens: 2000, output_tokens: 600}. Call _parse_codex_output and assert result.input_tokens == 2000, result.output_tokens == 600, result.cache_read_tokens is None, result.cache_create_tokens is None.

Update _parse_codex_output in orchestrator/src/orchestrator/agents/invoke.py: in the AgentResult constructor call, add input_tokens=total_input_tokens or None, output_tokens=total_output_tokens or None, cache_read_tokens=None, cache_create_tokens=None. The 'or None' converts 0 to None when no tokens were reported (empty output case), preserving the None-means-unavailable convention.

Write test in orchestrator/tests/test_invoke.py: TestParseGeminiTokens — create a _SubprocessResult with Gemini JSON including stats {input_tokens: 3000, output_tokens: 1200, turns: 1}. Call _parse_gemini_output and assert result.input_tokens == 3000, result.output_tokens == 1200, result.cache_read_tokens is None, result.cache_create_tokens is None.

Update _parse_gemini_output in orchestrator/src/orchestrator/agents/invoke.py: in the AgentResult constructor call, add input_tokens=input_tokens or None, output_tokens=output_tokens or None, cache_read_tokens=None, cache_create_tokens=None.

design_decisions.decision: Token fields are Optional[int] (int | None) defaulting to None, not int defaulting to 0
design_decisions.rationale: None signals 'backend did not report this metric' vs 0 meaning 'zero tokens used'. This is important for cache fields on codex/gemini which genuinely don't support cache reporting, and for Claude output from older CLI versions that lack usage blocks.

design_decisions.decision: Codex/Gemini tests go in orchestrator/tests/test_invoke.py, not shared/tests/test_cli_invoke.py
design_decisions.rationale: shared cannot import orchestrator (orchestrator depends on shared, not vice versa). Putting codex/gemini parser tests in shared/tests would create a circular dependency. The task description says 'shared/tests' but the parsers live in orchestrator, so we follow the architectural boundary.

design_decisions.decision: Field names use shortened cache_read_tokens / cache_create_tokens rather than matching the CLI's cache_read_input_tokens / cache_creation_input_tokens
design_decisions.rationale: The task description specifies these exact field names. The mapping from CLI names to shorter AgentResult names is documented in the parsing code.

design_decisions.decision: Use 'or None' conversion for codex/gemini zero-token edge case
design_decisions.rationale: When codex/gemini produce no parseable output, internal accumulators stay at 0. Converting 0 to None keeps the invariant that None means 'not meaningfully reported'. A successful run with actual 0 tokens is practically impossible, so this is safe.

reuse.how: All test parsers construct _SubprocessResult instances as input to the parse functions. Import from shared.cli_invoke.
Existing helper for creating AgentResult instances in tests. May need updating to support new fields but serves as the pattern for test fixtures.
Both parsers already compute total_input_tokens/total_output_tokens internally for cost estimation. We reuse these values directly as the new AgentResult fields instead of re-parsing.
Follow the existing TestCapHitBackoff class pattern for organizing related tests under a class with the asyncio marker.
